note: Largely flat, paved central parks with step-free paths, benches and the swan boats; some gentle slopes and a few brick sections but broadly easy for strollers and wheels.

A 2.5-mile route past historic sites; much of it follows uneven brick and cobbled sidewalks with hills through Beacon Hill and the North End, and some sites have steps. Doable in flat segments; tiring overall.
Fully accessible with lifts, step-free entrances, loaner wheelchairs and accessible toilets. Flat galleries throughout.
The century-old ballpark has accessible seating and entrances but tight concourses, ramps and some stairs to older sections; accessible seats need advance booking.

Postcard-perfect but built on a steep hill paved in uneven cobblestones and brick with the famous Acorn Street impassable for wheels; very hard for wheelchairs, strollers and seniors. Admire the flatter Charles Street below.
Cambridge's square and much of Harvard Yard are flat with paved paths and ramped building access, though some historic buildings have steps; broadly manageable.
The modern wing and courtyard are fully accessible with lifts; the historic palace galleries are largely accessible with a few tight spots. Step-free entry.
The modern waterfront and Harborwalk are flat, paved and fully step-free with ramps; easy and scenic for wheeling and strolling.
The marketplace is largely at ground level with accessible entrances and a flat central promenade, though the cobbled surrounds and crowds add friction. Manageable.
The beloved food quarter has narrow, hilly, cobbled streets and many restaurants with a step at the door; the food is worth it but access is a squeeze. Some flatter main streets.
Accessible with lifts and a spiral ramp around the central tank, step-free entry and accessible toilets; good for families though the ramp is long.
